items.content: University of Ghana Library Online Portal. Kwame uses this extensively for academic research papers and economic journals crucial for his thesis and startup market analysis. It's his primary source for scholarly economic literature.

African Development Bank (AfDB) Data Portal. Essential for accessing macroeconomic data, reports, and policy documents relevant to Ghana and West African economies. Kwame frequently references AfDB statistics for his newsletter and startup planning.

Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive). Kwame relies on this suite for drafting his newsletter, managing startup finances and project plans, and collaborating with his co-founder. Cloud storage ensures accessibility from anywhere.

WhatsApp Business. Used daily to communicate with smallholder farmers, relay market prices, and coordinate logistics for his agri-tech startup. Also used for informal communication with potential premium buyers.

Rhodes Trust Website. Kwame frequently visits this site to review scholarship criteria, application deadlines, and success stories. It serves as a constant reminder and motivator for his academic and leadership aspirations.
Substack Platform. Where Kwame hosts and distributes his weekly economics newsletter. He uses it to track subscriber growth, engage with readers through comments, and manage his content publication.

Prof. Adwoa Serwaa (University of Ghana Economics Department). Kwame's academic advisor and mentor. He regularly consults her for guidance on his thesis, startup strategy, and potential postgraduate applications, including the Rhodes Scholarship.
